assertions.description: Output contains all required sections: Film Details, Logline, Short Synopsis (50 words), Medium Synopsis (150 words), Long Synopsis (400 words), Production Notes, Key Subjects, About the Director, Credits
Three synopsis lengths are genuinely different in scope — not just truncated versions of each other
All sections are publication-ready with no placeholders or internal-document language, and output includes a Next Step note
Output includes a header block with source, start TC, frame rate, and an estimation disclaimer about accuracy
Timecodes progress sequentially, are in correct format for the frame rate (FF values within valid range), and do not split mid-sentence
Speaker labels are consistent throughout and timecodes are placed at the intervals specified by the user (speaker changes, fixed intervals, or both)
Output contains tiered sections: Structural Notes, Sequence-Level Notes, Pacing Notes, Narrative/Character Notes, and Positive Observations
Every note is paired with a specific suggested fix — not just identification of problems but actionable solutions
Notes are numbered for reference, in priority order, and output includes a Next Step note identifying the single highest-priority change
Output subtitle blocks have maximum 42 characters per line and maximum 2 lines per block (for broadcast standard)
Line breaks occur at grammatical boundaries — not mid-phrase — and proper nouns are corrected where auto-generation produced errors
Output includes a Changes Log that records every correction made with the reason for each change
